_meta.purpose: Westgard multirule QC -> trigger, error type, and action (warning vs reject). Feeds the AI + RAG so QC violations are explained with the right corrective action. The QC engine (backend quality) enforces; this KB explains. Decision support.

1_2s.name: 1-2s
1_2s.interpretation: WARNING only — do NOT reject the run; use it as a trigger to inspect the reject rules below.
1_2s.note: Rejecting on 1-2s alone gives ~5% false rejection per level.

2_2s.name: 2-2s
2_2s.trigger: Two consecutive results outside the same mean ± 2SD (within a run across levels, or across runs for one level)

r_4s.name: R-4s
r_4s.trigger: Range between two controls in a run exceeds 4SD (one at +2SD, the other at -2SD)

4_1s.name: 4-1s
4_1s.trigger: Four consecutive results outside the same mean ± 1SD
4_1s.note: Often a warning-level rule; investigate trend.

10x.name: 10x
10x.trigger: Ten consecutive results on the same side of the mean

2of3_2s.name: 2of3-2s
2of3_2s.trigger: Two of three consecutive results outside the same mean ± 2SD

3_1s.name: 3-1s
3_1s.trigger: Three consecutive results outside the same mean ± 1SD

6x.name: 6x
6x.trigger: Six consecutive results on the same side of the mean

7t.name: 7-T (trend)
7t.trigger: Seven consecutive results trending in the same direction

random_error.name: Random error (1-3s, R-4s)
random_error.note: Poor precision — look at the analytical step, not calibration.

systematic_error.name: Systematic error (2-2s, 4-1s, 10x, trends)
systematic_error.note: Bias/shift/drift — recalibrate and re-run controls before releasing patients.
